controllability.def: 可控性、可管理性的人物 (單數)
controllability.ex: The new wings improved flight controllability.

controllable.def: 可控制的人的人物 (單數)
controllable.ex: The problem was no longer easily controllable.

controlled.def: 受控制的、克制的人的人 (或物)
controlled.ex: The seeds were grown in a controlled state.

controller.def: 控制器、管理者、主管、主計官人物 (單數)
controller.ex: The air traffic controller guided the plane.

controlling.ex: He holds a controlling interest in the firm.

controversial.def: 爭議的、引起爭辯的人的人物 (爭)
controversial.ex: He is a highly controversial figure today.

controversialist.def: 善辯者、爭論者人物 (單數)
controversialist.ex: The famed controversialist spoke at hall.

controversy.def: 爭議、爭論、辯論人物 (單數)
controversy.ex: The new law caused a lot of controversy.

controvert.def: 反駁、爭論、否定的人的人
controvert.ex: They wanted to controvert his latest claim.

contumacious.def: 頑抗的、藐視法庭的人的人物
contumacious.ex: The contumacious prisoner refused to speak.

contumacy.ex: He was found guilty of extreme contumacy.

contumelious.ex: She spoke in an incredibly contumelious tone.

contumely.ex: He was treated with contumely and scorn.

contuse.ex: The hard fall managed to contuse his elbow.

contusion.def: 挫傷、青腫、瘀傷的人物 (單數)
contusion.ex: He had a bad contusion on his left leg.

conundrum.def: 謎題、難解的問題、冷笑話的人物 (單數) (謎)
conundrum.ex: It's a moral conundrum for all of us today.

conurbation.def: 大都市圈、城市群人物 (單數)
conurbation.ex: The city has became a vast local conurbation.

convalesce.ex: He spent a month to convalesce at home.

convalescence.def: 康復期、漸癒的人物 (單數)
convalescence.ex: She had a long period of convalescence now.

convalescent.def: 康復中的、恢復期病人的人的人物 (單數)
convalescent.ex: He is now staying in a local convalescent home.

convection.ex: Thermal convection causes the wind to blow.

convene.def: 召集、集合、傳喚的人的人
convene.ex: They decided to convene a meeting tonight.

convenience.def: 方便、便利設施、公廁的人物 (單數) (便)
convenience.col: at your convenience, convenience store, for convenience
convenience.ex: I'll call you again at your earliest convenience.

convenient.ex: The hotel is in a very highly convenient location.

conveniently.ex: The shop is conveniently situated near school.

convent.def: 女修道院、女修會人物 (單數)
convent.ex: She left the world and entered a convent.

convention.def: 大會、慣例、協定、習俗的人物 (單數) (慣)
convention.col: social convention, geneva convention, political convention
convention.ex: He has no respect for social local conventions.

conventional.def: 常規的、習慣的、傳統地 (俗)
conventional.ex: He has a very conventional view of the world.

conventionalism.def: 習俗主義、慣例的人物 (單數)
conventionalism.ex: The artist hated all forms of conventionalism.

conventionalist.def: 守舊者、習俗主義者人物 (單數)
conventionalist.ex: He is a staunch conventionalist in dressing.

conventionality.def: 習俗、慣例、常規的人物 (單數)

conventionalize.def: 使規例化、使習常的人的人
conventionalize.ex: The artist tried to conventionalize the style.

conventionally.ex: She was conventionally dressed for dinner.

converge.def: 匯合、收斂、聚焦的人的人
converge.ex: All roads converge at the city center today.

convergence.ex: We are seeing a technological convergence now.

convergent.def: 匯合的人、收斂的人的人物
convergent.ex: Convergent thinking leads to a single solution.

conversable.def: 易交談的人、易親近地 (友)
conversable.ex: He is a very highly conversable and kind man.

conversance.ex: His close conversance with law was great.

conversancy.ex: She has a general conversancy with tools.

conversant.def: 精通的人、熟悉的人的人物
conversant.ex: You must be conversant with the local rules.

conversation.col: have a conversation, casual conversation, conversation starter
conversation.ex: We had a long local conversation over coffee.

conversational.def: 對話的、口語的人的人物
conversational.ex: The lesson focused on conversational skills.

conversationalist.ex: He was a brilliant and witty conversationalist.

conversazione.def: 談笑會、藝文聚會人物 (單數)
conversazione.ex: They held a select conversazione at the club.

converse.def: 交談、相反的、逆向的人的人人物 (單數)
converse.col: the converse is true, converse with
converse.ex: They managed to converse in French all night.

conversely.ex: Dark clothes absorb heat, and conversely.

conversion.def: 轉換、換算、皈依人物 (單數)
conversion.ex: The rate of data conversion was quite slow.

convert.def: 轉換、皈依、換算的人的人人物 (單數)
convert.ex: The hotel is a quick convert from a local bank.

converter/convertor.def: 轉換者、轉流器、變頻器人物 (單數)
converter/convertor.ex: A catalytic converter reduces gas emission.

convertibility.ex: The currency convertibility is high tonight.

convertible.def: 可轉換的、敞篷車人物 (單數) (敞)
convertible.ex: He drives a beautiful expensive red convertible.

convex.def: 凸的、凸面的人的人物 (單數) (凸)
convex.ex: A convex lens makes objects look much larger.
